message:
fix(agent,serve,tmux,watcher,coordinator,process): harden agent type normalization, fix idempotency scoping, improve daemon lifecycle, and eliminate test flakiness

Agent type normalization was inconsistent across the codebase: NeedsDoubleEnter()
in agent/types.go compared raw enum values directly, meaning user-facing aliases
like "codex", "gemini", or " CodEx " would silently fall through to the default
case and return false. This caused agents spawned with non-canonical type strings
to miss the double-Enter quirk workaround, leading to dropped prompts in Codex
and Gemini TUI panes. The fix applies case-insensitive trimmed matching in
NeedsDoubleEnter(), and mirrors the same canonicalAgentType() normalization in
tmux/session.go's needsBufferSend() so that buffer-based paste is correctly
triggered for aliases like "codex" and "gemini" (not just "cod" and "gmi").
The swarm auto_respawner.normalizeAgentType() gets the same ToLower+TrimSpace
treatment for consistency. The prompt_injector_test expectations are updated to
reflect that aliases now correctly inherit double-Enter behavior. The
completion_filter_test updates the empty-string agent type expectation from
"user" to "unknown" to match the actual AgentTypeToString return value.

The robot/bulk_assign module gains a SendKeysForAgent dependency alongside the
existing SendKeys, so that bulk assignment prompt delivery respects agent-type-
specific send behavior (buffer paste for Codex multiline, etc.). When a caller
provides SendKeys without SendKeysForAgent, a shim is auto-generated that
discards the agent type and delegates to SendKeys, preserving backward compat.
The new bulkAssignTMUXAgentType() maps the robot layer's string agent types to
tmux.AgentType constants. Tests verify that the agent-aware path is preferred
when available and that the legacy SendKeys-only path still works.

The idempotency middleware in serve/server.go had a critical scoping bug: cache
keys were derived solely from the raw Idempotency-Key header value, meaning the
same key used across different HTTP methods or URL paths would replay the wrong
cached response. scopedIdempotencyKey() now builds the cache key from
Method + Path + Query + raw key, preventing cross-endpoint collisions. The
idempotency cache entry now also stores a subset of response headers (Content-Type
and X-Request-ID) and replays them on cache hits via cloneReplayHeaders() and
applyReplayHeaders(), so that replayed responses carry the original request ID
rather than the second caller's request ID. Tests cover same-key-different-path,
same-key-different-method, and request-ID preservation on replay.

The memory daemon lifecycle in serve/cass.go was fire-and-forget: if the daemon
process exited unexpectedly, its PID file lingered and the in-memory state still
reported "running". A new watchMemoryDaemonExit() goroutine waits on cmd.Wait(),
cleans up the PID file, transitions state to stopped, and publishes a
memory.daemon.failed or memory.daemon.stopped event. The stop handler now goes
through a proper stopMemoryDaemonProcess() that sends SIGINT, polls for exit with
waitForProcessExit(), escalates to Kill if needed, and uses a transient
DaemonStateStopping state so the exit watcher doesn't race against the explicit
stop path. removeMemoryDaemonPIDFileIfMatches() validates the PID in the JSON
matches the expected process before removing, preventing stale-PID file removal
from colliding with a new daemon instance. Tests use real subprocess helpers
(TestServeMemoryDaemonHelperProcess) to verify interrupt-based stop and zombie PID
file cleanup.

The checkpoint/restore module now validates that the working directory is not just
present but is actually a directory (not a plain file). A new ErrWorkingDirInvalid
sentinel is returned when stat succeeds but IsDir() is false. The HTTP layer in
serve/checkpoints.go maps both ErrDirectoryNotFound and ErrWorkingDirInvalid to
400 Bad Request. ValidateCheckpoint() reports more descriptive issue strings via
workingDirectoryIssue(). Tests cover the not-a-directory case for both restore and
validate paths.

The coordinator assignment flow previously silently succeeded (result.Success=true)
when the mail client was nil or the agent had no AgentMailName, meaning assignments
appeared to go through but no message was ever delivered. The fix makes both
conditions explicit early-return errors with descriptive messages, and moves
result.Success=true to after the successful SendMessage call. The event emission
switch in emitEvent() reorders cases so that agent.recovered (error->non-error
transition) is checked before agent.idle, preventing a recovering agent from being
misclassified as merely idle. The updateAgentStates() method extracts tmux calls
behind package-level function variables so tests can inject stubs without needing
a real tmux server. The seenPanes map is pre-sized and pre-populated from the
pane list rather than being populated during the update loop, which was
accidentally skipping the first update for each pane.

Process liveness detection (process/liveness.go) now reads /proc/<pid>/status and
parses the State: field to detect zombie (Z), dead (X/x) processes, returning
false for them instead of true. Previously, the mere existence of /proc/<pid>/status
was treated as proof of liveness, causing zombie processes to appear alive. The
test spawns a real child process, waits for it to reach zombie state, and verifies
IsAlive returns false.

The resilience monitor's restartAgent() increments RestartCount before the send
attempt rather than after, so that the log message accurately reports the attempt
number and the max-restarts comparison is correct even if the send fails. Four
tests that were flaky due to racing on async goroutines now call m.wg.Wait()
before assertions.

The TUI dashboard's Update handler for SessionDataWithOutputMsg was dropping
accumulated cmds (like Ollama PS refresh) when constructing warmupCmds, causing
Ollama memory display to never update after initial load. The fix appends cmds
to warmupCmds. Similarly, StatusUpdateMsg was returning only followUp and
fetchHealthCmd, dropping any cmds accumulated earlier in the handler; now it
appends both to the cmds slice and batches them. The local_perf_test for Ollama PS
refresh-when-fresh was asserting that a cmd is returned and executing it, but the
correct behavior is that no cmd should be returned when the cache is fresh (the
fetch was a no-op that returned an error, masking the logic bug). The success test
now correctly applies the OllamaPSResultMsg.Memory to the model.

The file reservation watcher previously called tmux.ListSessions() which returns
session metadata without pane details, then iterated over session.Panes which was
always empty. It now uses the pane-native GetAllPanesContext() API via injectable
function fields (listAllPanes, capturePaneOutput), enabling proper unit testing
without a live tmux server. A new WithSessionFilter() option restricts scanning
to a single tmux session, preventing the dashboard watcher from accidentally
processing panes belonging to other concurrent NTM sessions (which could cause
spurious reservation conflicts in multi-session environments). The dashboard CLI
passes the session name as the filter. Tests verify both the agent-pane-only
scanning and the session filtering behavior using injected stubs.

The tmux PaneStreamer and StreamManager constructors now default a nil *Client to
DefaultClient instead of panicking on nil pointer dereference, making them safer
for callers who don't need a custom client. Tests verify the nil-client defaulting.
The FIFODir default path was changed from a hardcoded /tmp path to an XDG-compliant
location under $XDG_STATE_HOME, and tests use t.Setenv to set XDG_STATE_HOME so
they don't depend on the host machine's directory structure.

The test utilities gain a tryWithGlobalTmuxTestLock() that attempts a non-blocking
flock (LOCK_NB) and returns false if the lock is busy, instead of blocking
indefinitely. KillAllTestSessionsSilent() uses this try-lock so that concurrent
test packages in multi-agent CI don't deadlock waiting for each other's TestMain
cleanup. The Windows stub unconditionally succeeds. The safety_policy_test
expectation for DCG-missing + approval-required is corrected from exitCode=0 to
exitCode=1, matching the actual unified exit code semantics from a prior fix.

Beads issue tracker synced with new tracking entries for the idempotency scoping
fix and related work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -103,7 +103,7 @@ func TestAgentTypeToString(t *testing.T) {
 		{"claude", tmux.AgentClaude, "claude"},
 		{"codex", tmux.AgentCodex, "codex"},
 		{"gemini", tmux.AgentGemini, "gemini"},
-		{"empty string type", tmux.AgentType(""), "user"},
+		{"empty string type", tmux.AgentType(""), "unknown"},
 		{"custom type", tmux.AgentType("aider"), "aider"},
 	}

@@ -755,15 +755,23 @@ func (m *Monitor) restartAgent(ctx context.Context, agent *AgentState) {
 		return
 	}
 
+	m.mu.Lock()
+	var attemptRestartCount int
+	if a, ok := m.agents[agent.PaneID]; ok {
+		a.RestartCount++
+		attemptRestartCount = a.RestartCount
+	}
+	m.mu.Unlock()
+
 	if err := sendFunc(agent.PaneID, paneCmd, true); err != nil {
-		log.Printf("[resilience] Failed to restart agent %s: %v", agent.PaneID, err)
+		log.Printf("[resilience] Failed to restart agent %s (attempt %d/%d): %v",
+			agent.PaneID, attemptRestartCount, m.cfg.Resilience.MaxRestarts, err)
 		return
 	}
 
 	m.mu.Lock()
 	var finalRestartCount int
 	if a, ok := m.agents[agent.PaneID]; ok {
-		a.RestartCount++
 		a.Healthy = true
 		a.LastRestart = time.Now()
 		finalRestartCount = a.RestartCount

@@ -1298,8 +1298,10 @@ func TestNormalizeAgentType(t *testing.T) {
 		{"claude-code", "cc"},
 		{"cod", "cod"},
 		{"codex", "cod"},
+		{" CodEx ", "cod"},
 		{"gmi", "gmi"},
 		{"gemini", "gmi"},
+		{" GEMINI ", "gmi"},
 		{"unknown", "unknown"},
 	}

@@ -1408,6 +1408,18 @@ func TestNeedsBufferSend(t *testing.T) {
 			content:   "",
 			want:      false,
 		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex alias with newline",
+			agentType: AgentType("codex"),
+			content:   "line1\nline2",
+			want:      true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex alias long content no newline",
+			agentType: AgentType("codex"),
+			content:   strings.Repeat("a", 513),
+			want:      true,
+		},
 		// Claude cases - uses buffer for multi-line content
 		{
 			name:      "claude single line",
@@ -1427,6 +1439,24 @@ func TestNeedsBufferSend(t *testing.T) {
 			content:   strings.Repeat("x", 1000),
 			want:      false,
 		},
+		{
+			name:      "claude alias with newline",
+			agentType: AgentType("claude"),
+			content:   "line1\nline2",
+			want:      true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "gemini alias with newline",
+			agentType: AgentType("gemini"),
+			content:   "line1\nline2",
+			want:      true,
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "codex alias trimmed and cased",
+			agentType: AgentType(" CodEx "),
+			content:   strings.Repeat("z", 513),
+			want:      true,
+		},
 		// User pane - never uses buffer
 		{
 			name:      "user single line",

@@ -29,6 +29,7 @@ func NewFileReservationWatcherFromConfig(
 	client *agentmail.Client,
 	projectDir string,
 	agentName string,
+	sessionName string,
 	conflictCallback ConflictCallback,
 ) *FileReservationWatcher {
 	if !cfg.Enabled {
@@ -39,6 +40,7 @@ func NewFileReservationWatcherFromConfig(
 		WithWatcherClient(client),
 		WithProjectDir(projectDir),
 		WithAgentName(agentName),
+		WithSessionFilter(sessionName),
 		WithDebug(cfg.Debug),
 	}
